_meta.source: ux-skill v2 - Python pivot (ported from bin/ux-lint.sh)

entries.name: Inter used as display font
entries.why: Inter is a body font tuned for screen legibility at small sizes; deployed as display it reads as the default startup-landing fingerprint.
entries.fix: Pair Inter (body) with a distinctive display face: Geist, Satoshi, Cabinet Grotesk, General Sans, Outfit, or a brand-specific variable sans.

entries.name: Default purple-to-blue AI gradient
entries.why: Purple-to-blue gradient on white is the strongest visual fingerprint of unconstrained model output and reads as template-marketplace AI slop.
entries.fix: Use a single restrained accent (Emerald, Electric Blue, Deep Rose, Amber) against neutrals; keep gradient hue spread under 60 degrees if used at all.

entries.name: Three equal cards in a row
entries.why: Three equal cards with three icons and three short paragraphs is the safest default the generator reaches for and the strongest layout fingerprint in AI-generated marketing surfaces.
entries.fix: Use asymmetric layouts: bento grids, 2-and-1 splits, 4 with one spanning width. Vary card size by content priority.

entries.name: Generic placeholder names
entries.why: John/Jane Doe and their cousins signal that nobody thought about who would actually use the product - immediate AI-generated-tutorial vibe.
entries.fix: Use plausible names that fit the target market: Maya Iqbal, Adam Levin, Wen Zhang, Layla Haddad. Match region for regional products.

entries.name: Lorem ipsum in shipping code
entries.why: Lorem ipsum in production source code is unshipped placeholder content - the most obvious draft-state leak.
entries.fix: Write real copy that makes a specific claim about the product. If you can't write it yet, mark the block with TODO and a content owner.

entries.name: Emoji used as UI element
entries.why: Emojis render inconsistently across platforms, ignore brand color, and signal informality where SVG icons signal craft.
entries.fix: Inline SVG from Lucide, Feather, Phosphor, or Heroicons at 1.5-2px stroke with currentColor. Whitelist U+2713 check and U+2318 command only.

entries.name: Centered hero composition
entries.why: Centered headline + centered subtitle + centered button stack is the laziest hero composition and signals the generator picked it when it couldn't find a better layout.
entries.fix: Use left-aligned hero with editorial 7-5 or 8-4 grid split; let the imagery earn its own column.

entries.name: Multi-stop gradient text
entries.why: Three-stop rainbow text on a hero word is the strongest 'AI hero' tell after the purple-to-blue gradient.
entries.fix: Use a single solid color for headlines. If gradient text is essential, two stops in analogous hues at modest saturation.

entries.name: Purple glow shadow on cards
entries.why: Purple-tinted shadows on cards are the second-strongest 'AI premium' fingerprint after the gradient itself.
entries.fix: Use neutral diffusion shadow: shadow-[0_20px_40px_-15px_rgba(0,0,0,0.08)]. Color comes from the surface, not the shadow.

entries.name: Default 300ms transition timing
entries.why: 300ms is the editor default - using it everywhere signals zero motion intent and reads as laziness to anyone who tunes animation curves.
entries.fix: Micro-interactions 150-220ms, complex transitions 250-400ms, exit 60-70% of entry. Pick durations that match the feel, not the default.

entries.name: Material default easing everywhere
entries.why: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) is Material Design's default standard ease - using it as the only curve signals no taste decision was made.
entries.fix: Use cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for premium exits, cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) for playful overshoot, or spring physics.

entries.name: System font stack with no chosen typeface
entries.why: Falling back to the system stack alone with no chosen typeface gives up the chance to have any typographic identity.
entries.fix: Pick a real face: Geist, Inter, Satoshi, IBM Plex Sans, Cabinet Grotesk, or the brand's variable. System stack is the absolute last fallback.

entries.name: Emoji used as icon stamp
entries.why: Emoji where an SVG icon belongs breaks brand color, scales poorly, and renders differently on every OS.
entries.fix: Inline SVG icon with currentColor and a consistent stroke width across the surface.

entries.name: rounded-full applied to everything
entries.why: rounded-full on every button and input is the iOS-tutorial fingerprint - signals the radius decision was skipped entirely.
entries.fix: rounded-full for pills, avatars, icon buttons only. Use rounded-md or rounded-lg for primary buttons; rounded-xl or rounded-2xl for cards.

entries.name: Arbitrary hero font size
entries.why: Arbitrary px values like text-[90px] or text-[112px] bypass the type scale and signal the size was picked by eye, not by system.
entries.fix: Extend the Tailwind theme scale or use clamp() for fluid hero typography. text-6xl/text-7xl/text-8xl exist for a reason.

entries.name: Backdrop blur with no glass surface
entries.why: Backdrop blur applied without a translucent surface behind it is GPU work for zero visual benefit - decoration, not design.
entries.fix: Pair backdrop-blur with a translucent fill: bg-white/70 or bg-zinc-950/60. Otherwise remove the blur entirely.

entries.name: Generic overlapping avatar stack
entries.why: Three overlapping circular avatars in the nav as 'our team' or 'join 10k users' is a content-marketing template tell.
entries.fix: Use real customer logos, named testimonials with quotes, or specific signal (Used by 312 engineering teams at Series-A companies).

entries.name: Hardcoded five-star testimonial
entries.why: Five hardcoded stars next to a fake quote is the lowest-trust pattern on the web; users have learned to discount it on sight.
entries.fix: Show real, named, attributable testimonials with company affiliation and a specific outcome. No star count unless aggregated from a real review system.

entries.name: Bouncing arrow on CTA
entries.why: Bouncing arrow on a CTA is the desperate-attention pattern that signals the copy itself isn't doing the work.
entries.fix: Let the CTA copy carry the action (Start a 14-day trial, Read the deployment guide). If you need an arrow, use a static SVG that nudges 2-4px on hover.

entries.name: Hamburger menu on desktop
entries.why: Hamburger on a wide viewport hides nav from users for no reason; signals the responsive breakpoint was skipped.
entries.fix: Show inline nav on md: and up; hide the hamburger above that breakpoint with md:hidden.

entries.name: Named Tailwind colors with no semantic token
entries.why: Raw bg-blue-500 / bg-purple-500 with no semantic token (primary, success, danger) signals the color system was never designed.
entries.fix: Define semantic tokens in tailwind.config or CSS custom properties: bg-primary, text-success, ring-accent. Map them once.

entries.name: Inline style attribute
entries.why: Inline style= attributes bypass the design system, defeat caching, and signal the styling decision was made ad hoc.
entries.fix: Use Tailwind utilities, a CSS class, or a styled component. Reserve inline style for runtime-computed values (animated translate, dynamic CSS vars) only.

entries.name: console.log in component code
entries.why: console.log shipped to production leaks state to anyone with DevTools open and signals the build didn't go through a real review.
entries.fix: Remove debug logs before shipping. If you need structured logging, use a logger module that's tree-shaken out of production builds.

entries.name: TODO or FIXME in shipping code
entries.why: TODO/FIXME in shipping code is an unresolved promise to the reader and a draft-state leak.
entries.fix: Resolve the TODO before merge, or move it to the issue tracker with a specific owner and date.

entries.name: TypeScript any type
entries.why: any defeats the type system and signals the type was never figured out - the code is JavaScript pretending to be TypeScript.
entries.fix: Use unknown for genuinely unknown shapes and narrow with type guards. Use a specific interface or generic for everything else.

entries.name: SVG without aria-label or aria-hidden
entries.why: SVG with no aria-label and no aria-hidden is announced by screen readers as 'graphic' with no context - failure of WCAG 1.1.1.
entries.fix: Decorative SVG gets aria-hidden="true". Informative SVG gets aria-label="description" or aria-labelledby pointing to a title element.

entries.name: Button missing type attribute
entries.why: A <button> inside a <form> without type="button" defaults to type="submit" and silently submits the form on every click.
entries.fix: Always set type="button" or type="submit" explicitly. The default is a footgun, not a feature.

entries.name: Image missing alt attribute
entries.why: Missing alt breaks screen readers, SEO image indexing, and the broken-image fallback all at once - WCAG 1.1.1 failure.
entries.fix: Every gets alt. Decorative images get alt="" (empty). Informative images get a description a screen reader user would need.

entries.name: Anchor with onClick but no href
entries.why: An <a> without href is not focusable by default and is invisible to assistive tech - it looks like a link but behaves like a button.
entries.fix: Use <button> for actions. Use <a href="..."> for navigation. Never an anchor without href as a click target.

entries.name: Skipped heading level
entries.why: Heading hierarchy gaps (h1 then h3, or h3 with no h1/h2 preceding) break the screen-reader landmark tree and the SEO outline.
entries.fix:
h1 once per page; nest h2/h3/h4 in order. If you need a small visual heading without a logical level, use a styled

instead.

entries.name: Default shadcn token block unmodified
entries.why: The default shadcn HSL token block with --radius 0.5rem is recognizable in two seconds by anyone who has shipped one shadcn app.
entries.fix: Customize tokens at init: swap slate for zinc/stone, shift primary to a brand hue, set --radius to 0.625rem or 0.75rem.

entries.name: Purple-pink mesh gradient hero
entries.why: Purple-to-pink mesh gradient is the second-most fingerprinted AI-hero pattern after blue-to-purple - 'I generated a landing page' aesthetic.
entries.fix: Single accent against neutral canvas. If a hero needs depth, layer subtle noise or a single low-saturation analogous gradient under 60deg hue spread.

entries.name: Low-contrast text on card
entries.why: Dark text token (zinc-500/600/700) on a dark surface fails WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast and signals the dark mode pairing was never tested.
entries.fix: Test contrast on both themes. On dark surfaces use text-zinc-100 to text-zinc-300; reserve 500-700 for tertiary captions on light surfaces.

entries.name: Lazy z-index value
entries.why: z-index: 9999 is the 'I gave up on the stacking system' value - signals there's no z-scale token map and stacking bugs are coming.
entries.fix: Define a z-scale token set (z-base: 0, z-dropdown: 10, z-sticky: 20, z-modal: 50, z-toast: 60). Use the tokens, never a raw 9999.

entries.name: Infinite scroll without keyboard fallback
entries.why: Pure infinite scroll with no Load more button locks out keyboard users and breaks back-button history - WCAG 2.4 failure plus a usability one.
entries.fix: Ship infinite scroll AND a visible Load more button. The button is the keyboard path; the observer is the convenience layer.

entries.name: Viewport blocks pinch-zoom
entries.why: user-scalable=no blocks pinch-zoom for low-vision users on touch devices - a WCAG 1.4.4 failure that locks out anyone who needs to enlarge text.
entries.fix: Remove user-scalable=no entirely, or set it to yes. Default viewport: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">.

entries.name: outline removed without focus-visible replacement
entries.why: outline: none removes the only keyboard focus indicator and leaves keyboard users with no signal of focus state - a WCAG 2.4.7 failure.
entries.fix: Pair outline: none with a :focus-visible rule that restores a visible ring: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--primary); outline-offset: 2px; }.

entries.name: Input placeholder used as the only label
entries.why: Placeholders disappear on focus, sit at low contrast by default, and are not announced consistently by assistive tech - using one as the only label fails WCAG 3.3.2.
entries.fix: Always pair <input> with a <label for="id"> or aria-label. Placeholder is for an optional hint, never the field name.

entries.name: Click handler on <div> without role or tabindex
entries.why: A <div onClick> is invisible to keyboard users and assistive tech - the action exists only for mouse users, a WCAG 2.1.1 and 4.1.2 failure.
entries.fix: Use <button onClick> for actions. If you must keep the <div>, add role="button" tabindex="0" and an onKeyDown handler for Enter and Space.

entries.name: Anchor styled as button without href
entries.why: An <a class="btn"> without href is not focusable, not in the tab order, and not announced as a link - looks like a button but behaves like nothing.
entries.fix: Use <button> for actions, <a href="..."> for navigation. If the destination is unknown until runtime, render a button or set href="#" with preventDefault.

entries.name: 100vh without 100dvh fallback for mobile
entries.why: 100vh on mobile includes the browser chrome that retracts on scroll, so the layout jumps as the address bar collapses - dynamic viewport units (dvh) fix this.
entries.fix: Use height: 100dvh or h-[100dvh] as the primary, with h-screen / 100vh as the legacy fallback for browsers that lack dvh.

entries.name: Title Case On Display Headings
entries.why: Marketing-style Title Case on every headline dates the design to a 2014 SaaS template and slows reading. Sentence case scales better and reads faster.
entries.fix: Default to sentence case for headlines. Reserve title case for proper nouns and brand names, or for an explicit editorial decision.

entries.name: Filler marketing verb in headline
entries.why: Elevate, Seamless, Unleash, Revolutionize, Empower, Supercharge, Transform - the unmistakable fingerprint of unedited marketing copy generated without a real product in mind.
entries.fix: Name the specific action and the specific outcome. Cut the verb if it isn't carrying weight. The reader knows what the product does only if you say what the product does.

entries.name: Generic CTA text (Click here, Learn more)
entries.why: Click here / Learn more / Get started reveals nothing about the destination and fails screen-reader scan-by-link - users hear a list of identical labels with no context.
entries.fix: Name the specific action: Read pricing, Start free trial, View dashboard, Open API docs. The link text should make sense out of context.

entries.name: Round number marketing stat
entries.why: 99.99%, 10x faster, 100% guaranteed - the suspiciously round numbers AI invents to sound credible. Real measurements are messy and specific.
entries.fix: Use the real number you measured: 99.93% uptime over Q1, 4.2x faster median build, refunded 218 of 244 reported issues. Specificity is the signal.

entries.name: Display heading at 700+ font-weight
entries.why: Display fonts at 700 weight read dense and visually muddy. Most display faces are drawn to sing at 500-600; 700+ is for body emphasis, not headlines.
entries.fix: Use font-weight 500 or 600 on display headings. Reserve 700+ for body emphasis (bold inline text, table totals, primary CTA labels).

entries.name: ALL CAPS at body-text size or larger
entries.why: ALL CAPS reading speed is 13-20% slower than mixed case because word shape is lost. At 14px or larger it actively impedes reading.
entries.fix: Reserve uppercase for eyebrows, labels, and badges under 14px. Use sentence case for anything readers need to read, not just scan.

entries.name: cursor:pointer on non-interactive element
entries.why: A <div> or <span> with cursor: pointer promises clickable behavior to sighted mouse users while keyboard and screen-reader users get nothing - a hidden interaction.
entries.fix: If it clicks, make it a <button> or <a href>. If you keep the <div>, add role and tabindex and a keydown handler - the cursor change is the smallest signal a real control owes.

entries.name: Transition on a layout property
entries.why: Animating width, height, top, left, margin, padding triggers layout and paint on every frame - the dropped-frame jank pattern that makes the UI feel cheap.
entries.fix: Animate transform (translate, scale) and opacity only. Use scale instead of width, translate instead of top/left. The compositor thanks you.

entries.name: Image without width and height attributes
entries.why: An without width and height attributes causes Cumulative Layout Shift as the image loads and the page reflows - the single biggest CLS regression on most sites.
entries.fix: Always set width and height (the intrinsic pixel dimensions) on every , even when styled with CSS. The browser uses them to reserve space.

entries.name: Lone emoji used as functional icon
entries.why: A bare emoji standing in for an icon renders inconsistently across platforms, ignores brand color, and is announced unpredictably by screen readers.
entries.fix: Use inline SVG (Lucide, Feather, Phosphor, Heroicons) at 1.5-2px stroke with currentColor. Add aria-label when the icon carries meaning.

entries.name: Chrome-y multi-stop gradient
entries.why: Three-plus color stops in a linear-gradient is the v0 / Cursor / generic-AI default; reads as chrome-y template output rather than a deliberate brand decision.
entries.fix: Use a two-stop gradient (or a single solid) with hue spread under 60 degrees. If depth is needed, layer a low-opacity overlay instead of stacking stops.

entries.name: Five-plus box-shadow layers stacked
entries.why: Stacking five or more shadow layers is the AI's idea of premium depth; reads as visual bloat and chews the GPU on paint.
entries.fix: Use one ambient diffusion shadow (e.g. 0 20px 40px -15px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)) and at most one tight contact shadow. Depth comes from contrast, not layer count.

entries.name: Pixel height on a text container
entries.why: Fixing a pixel height on a container that also styles text breaks reflow at every other zoom level and font scale - the layout is one breakpoint away from clipping.
entries.fix: Use min-height for floor protection, line-height + padding for vertical rhythm, and let content determine height. Never lock text height in px.

entries.name: Over-the-top hover scale
entries.why: scale(1.1) or higher on hover reads as amateur - it shouts when a 1-2 percent nudge or a shadow lift would be more confident.
entries.fix: Use scale(1.01) to scale(1.03), or replace the scale with a translateY(-2px) plus shadow shift. Restraint reads premium.

entries.name: Dynamic Island inner glow on cards
entries.why: iOS Dynamic Island's inner-glow + blur recipe is being copied as the default on every card and pill - reads as the 2024 AI-marketing-template tell.
entries.fix: If you want depth, use a single outer diffusion shadow tuned to the surface. Reserve inset glows for a deliberate metallic or pill-style accent, not every surface.

entries.name: backdrop-filter blur without background fallback
entries.why: backdrop-filter is unsupported on Firefox and older Safari - without a translucent background fallback, the surface renders fully transparent and content underneath bleeds through.
entries.fix: Always pair backdrop-filter: blur with a background-color (e.g. background: rgba(255,255,255,0.7)). The bg is the fallback; the blur is the upgrade.

entries.name: Default AI pricing tier amounts
entries.why: $9 / $19 / $29 / $49 / $99 / $199 are the prices AI reaches for when no real pricing exists - unconvincing placeholders that signal nobody priced the product.
entries.fix: Use the real price you charge, with the real currency and the real billing cadence. If pricing isn't set yet, mark the tier as TBD with an owner, not a placeholder.

entries.name: Unsourced trust-by claim
entries.why: Trusted by 1000+ teams / Used by Fortune 500 / Loved by 50k developers - unsourced trust claims that signal the generator invented the number to fill space.
entries.fix: Name the actual companies, link to the actual case studies, or show the actual review aggregate. Specificity beats magnitude every time.

entries.name: Fake just-now timestamp in static markup
entries.why: Just now / moments ago in static HTML is a fake activity signal - it stays Just now forever and signals nobody wired the real time-ago helper.
entries.fix: Render real timestamps via a time-ago helper bound to a real source (Date.now, server time, last_event_at). If the surface is static, use a real date string instead.

entries.name: Rainbow gradient on display text
entries.why: background-clip: text on a multi-stop linear-gradient is the AI hero's chrome-y rainbow word - the loudest signal that no taste decision was made on color.
entries.fix: Use one solid color on display text. If gradient text is essential, two stops in analogous hues at modest saturation, never three-plus rainbow stops.

entries.name: Emoji at start of list item
entries.why: An emoji at the start of every list item is the AI signature - check marks, sparkles, rockets, party poppers used as bullet markers signal a generator that confused decoration with hierarchy.
entries.fix: Use the native list disc, an SVG check icon, or a numeric counter. Reserve the U+2713 check and U+2318 command as allowed dingbats.

entries.name: Animated grain or noise without GPU hint
entries.why: Animated grain or noise without a will-change or transform hint forces a CPU repaint every frame and causes paint thrashing on low-end devices.
entries.fix: Add will-change: transform or compose the noise via an SVG <feTurbulence> overlay on a transformed layer. Or drop the animation - static grain reads the same and costs nothing.

entries.name: Unsourced large-number marketing claim
entries.why: We saved 10,000 hours / Generated 1,000,000 lines of code / Reduced 50,000 tickets - large round numbers with no citation that signal the magnitude was invented to sound impressive.
entries.fix: Use the real measured number with the real source: 'In Q1 2026, customers saved 4,217 support hours (mean across 38 teams)'. Real numbers are messy and specific.

entries.name: Magnitude-default before-after claim
entries.why: 0 to 100 / 100% guaranteed / 100% effective are the magnitude-by-default phrases AI reaches for - readers have learned to discount them.
entries.fix: Quote the real spread: 'from 14 manual steps to 3 automated'. If you can't measure it, don't claim it.

entries.name: Logo cloud image with empty alt
entries.why: An inside a Trusted by / logo-cloud section with alt="" is an unsourced trust claim with no machine-readable company name - the logo is decorative even when the surface is selling credibility.
entries.fix: Set alt to the real company name (alt="Stripe"), and only use the logo if you have permission. If you don't have permission, you don't have the trust signal.

entries.name: Load-bearing copy hidden in a title attribute
entries.why: A title attribute carrying 60+ characters of real content is hover-only - invisible on touch, inconsistently announced by screen readers, undiscoverable on keyboard. The information is effectively missing for most users.
entries.fix: Move the content into the visible flow or into an aria-describedby popover that is reachable by keyboard and touch. Reserve title for one-line, optional clarifications.

entries.name: Fixed scroll-to-top button on short pages
entries.why: A floating back-to-top button on a page under four viewports adds permanent visual noise for a vanishingly rare interaction - signals the generator included the pattern as decoration, not need.
entries.fix: Drop the button. If pages can grow past four viewports, gate the button on scroll position and hide it below threshold. Keyboard users have Home; mouse users have the wheel.

entries.name: Square aspect-ratio as default everywhere
entries.why: Every image clipped to 1:1 is the Instagram-grid AI fingerprint - signals the generator picked the safest crop instead of letting content set the frame. Real editorial layouts use varied ratios (3:2, 4:5, 16:9, 21:9) to create rhythm.
entries.fix: Vary aspect ratio by content: 3:2 for product shots, 4:5 for portraits, 16:9 for hero or video. Reserve 1:1 for genuine grid contexts (avatars, social previews).

entries.name: Repeating noise or grain texture overlay
entries.why: A noise.png or grain.svg tiled across a hero is the 2024 generative-UI fingerprint - signals 'I added texture' without a coherent material story. Most viewers register it as visual static, not depth.
entries.fix: If grain is a deliberate brand choice, generate it via SVG <feTurbulence> with low baseFrequency and 4-6% opacity. Otherwise drop the texture; flatness reads more confident than fake film grain.

entries.name: rounded-2xl or larger applied to everything
entries.why: rounded-2xl/3xl (24-32px+) on every card, button, and input is the Tailwind AI signature - signals the radius was set once and copy-pasted instead of being calibrated per element size. Buttons end up looking like pills, inputs lose definition, dense surfaces feel cartoonish.
entries.fix: Calibrate radius to element size: 4-8px for inputs, 8-12px for buttons, 12-16px for cards, 16-24px for marketing surfaces only. Define a 4-5 step radius scale and use the tokens.

entries.name: transition: all (lazy property list)
entries.why: transition: all is the lazy default that animates every property change - including layout-triggering ones like width/height/margin - causing jank and unintended motion. It also fires on properties the author never meant to transition.
entries.fix: List the exact properties: transition: transform 200ms ease, opacity 200ms ease. In Tailwind, prefer transition-colors, transition-opacity, transition-transform over transition-all.

entries.name: Backdrop-blur frosted-glass on four-plus surfaces
entries.why: Frosted-glass everywhere is the iOS-aesthetic AI tell - signals the generator confused 'depth' with 'blur every surface'. Four-plus backdrop-blur layers on a single page is the visual equivalent of using bold on every word.
entries.fix: Reserve glass for one or two surfaces that have a clear reason to feel translucent (a floating nav, a notification toast). Solid surfaces with restraint read more premium than four blurred panels.

entries.name: Tight letter-spacing on heavy display heading
entries.why: tracking-tighter (-0.05em) on font-weight 800+ display is the unmistakable 'AI hero' typographic recipe - copied from every Linear-clone landing page. The combination crushes the counters and reads as default, not deliberate.
entries.fix: Use letter-spacing -0.01em to -0.02em on display headings, with font-weight 500-600. Reserve heavy-and-tight pairings for short brand wordmarks, not full headlines.

entries.name: Card actions revealed only on hover
entries.why: Card actions hidden at opacity: 0 and revealed only on :hover are invisible to touch users, keyboard users, and anyone using assistive tech - the action does not exist for most of your audience. WCAG 2.1.1 and 1.4.13 failure.
entries.fix: Show card actions persistently at reduced visual weight (lower opacity, smaller scale, secondary color), or make them reachable via a visible focus-state. Hover is a progressive disclosure, never the only path.

entries.name: aria-hidden on a focusable interactive element
entries.why: aria-hidden="true" on a focusable element (button, link, input) removes it from the accessibility tree while leaving it in the tab order - keyboard users can land on a control that screen readers refuse to announce. WCAG 4.1.2 failure.
entries.fix: If the control is decorative, remove it from the DOM or set tabindex="-1" AND aria-hidden together. If it has a function, drop the aria-hidden and give it a proper accessible name via aria-label or visible text.

entries.name: Micro-interaction with animation-duration over 800ms
entries.why: A hover, focus, or state-change animation longer than 800ms feels sluggish - users perceive UI under 200ms as instant, under 500ms as snappy, over 800ms as broken or laggy. Long durations on micro-interactions signal no taste decision was made.
entries.fix: Cap micro-interactions at 150-400ms. Reserve longer durations (800ms+) for narrative scenes, page transitions, or deliberate orchestration - never for a button hover or icon flip.

entries.name: Tailwind text-3xl text-4xl text-5xl AI hero stack
entries.why: text-3xl md:text-4xl lg:text-5xl (and the 4xl/5xl/6xl shift) is the verbatim Tailwind tutorial cadence for every AI-generated hero - signals the responsive type was set by stepping through preset utilities, not by tuning the actual feel at each breakpoint.
entries.fix: Use clamp() for fluid type: text-[clamp(2.5rem,5vw,4.5rem)] or define a custom step in tailwind.config that jumps by content priority, not utility index.

entries.name: repeat(3, 1fr) as a default grid
entries.why: repeat(3, 1fr) with no minmax(), no auto-fit, and no asymmetric weighting is the laziest grid declaration - signals the layout was set by a generator that defaulted to 'three equal columns' without thinking about content priority or responsive behavior.
entries.fix: Use minmax for responsive floors: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr)). For deliberate three-column layouts, weight by content: grid-template-columns: 2fr 1fr 1fr, or use named-area grids.

entries.name: Unsplash photo URL with empty or missing alt
entries.why: An images.unsplash.com/photo-* URL with no alt (or alt="") is the stock-photo placeholder fingerprint - signals the generator pasted the first credible image without choosing it for the content. Screen-reader users hear nothing; the page reads as filler.
entries.fix: Replace with a real product or brand image and write an alt that describes the content (alt="Customer service team reviewing a dashboard"). If the image stays as decoration only, set alt="" deliberately AND justify why a stock photo earns a spot on the surface.

entries.name: Hardcoded v1.0.0 in nav or footer
entries.why: A static 'v1.0.0' in the nav or footer never updates with the real build - signals the generator added a version chip as decoration without wiring it to package.json or the deploy SHA. Worse, the badge stays at 1.0.0 forever and reads as stagnant.
entries.fix: Either pull the version from package.json at build time (Vite/Webpack define), bind it to the deploy SHA, or remove the chip entirely. A wrong version is worse than no version.

entries.name: Three-plus CTA buttons clustered in hero
entries.why: Three or more CTA-styled buttons within a single hero is decision paralysis - the AI's compromise when it can't choose the primary action. Conversion research is unanimous: one primary + one secondary outperforms three competing primaries.
entries.fix: Pick one primary CTA. Demote the rest to text links or move them to a secondary surface. If you genuinely need three actions, restructure the hero into a comparison or a stepped layout so the primary is unambiguous.

entries.name: Numbered placeholder class names (card-1, feature-2)
entries.why: card-1, feature-2, step-3 are the throwaway class names a generator emits when it has no semantic for what each block actually contains - signals nobody named the content. They also defeat CSS reuse because every variant gets a unique class.
entries.fix: Name classes by purpose: .pricing-card, .feature-comparison, .onboarding-step. If the cards really are interchangeable, use a single .card class with modifiers (.card--featured) or data attributes (data-tier="pro").

entries.name: target="_blank" without rel="noopener noreferrer"
entries.why: target="_blank" without rel="noopener" lets the opened page access window.opener and rewrite the source URL - a tabnabbing vector. It also blocks the browser's tab-recycle optimization. A11y-adjacent because hijacked navigation breaks the user's mental model of where they are.
entries.fix: Always pair target="_blank" with rel="noopener noreferrer". Better: ask whether the link actually needs a new tab - in-page navigation respects user choice and back-button history.

entries.name: Default CSS border-spin loader recipe
entries.why: The 4-border-color, border-top-different, animation: spin recipe is the textbook 1998 spinner that every AI generator emits for any loading state - signals the loading affordance was set by default, not designed. Modern systems use skeleton screens, progress bars, or branded indicators.
entries.fix: Replace with a skeleton screen for content loading, a progress indicator for known-duration tasks, or a branded SVG mark animated via transform: rotate. The bordered spinner is the loading equivalent of Lorem ipsum.
